zh.lifeSummary: 當Francis Chamberlain於1589年1月1日出生在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德時，他的父親Francis Chamberlain28歲，母親Jone Conias30歲。他於1619年與Agnes Haiden在英國結婚。他們至少是12個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1623年1月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓享年34歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Francis Chamberlain于1589年1月1日出生在英国英格兰艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德时，他的父亲Francis Chamberlain28岁，母亲Jone Conias30岁。他于1619年与Agnes Haiden在英国结婚。他们至少是12个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1623年1月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Chamberlain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name from Old French chambrelain, Norman French cambrelanc, cambrelen(c) ‘chamberlain’ (of ancient Germanic origin, from kamer ‘chamber, room’, Latin camera (see Chambers ) + the diminutive suffix -(l)ing). This was originally the name of an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, but is so widespread in late medieval England that it must sometimes have been used of people of more ordinary status, perhaps as a nickname for an officious or self-important person or for someone who played the role of chamberlain in a folk play, tableau, or ceremony. Compare Chancellor for a possible similar usage.

variants.name: Chamberlin
meanings: French: occupational name for an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, from Old French chamberlenc ‘chamberlain’. See also Chamberlain and compare above.

variants.name: Chambers
meanings: from Middle English chaumbre ‘room (in a house); reception room in a palace or official building’ (Old French chambre). It is identical in implied function with the Chamberlain , which denoted an official: to pay in cameram was to pay into the exchequer, of which the camerarius or chamberer was in charge. The surname also applied to clerks employed there. As the office of Chamberlain rose in the social scale, this term remained reserved for more humble servants of the bedchamber or private quarters.
(of Norman origin): habitational name from Les Chambres, Manche (France).

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Francis Chamberlain was born on 1 January 1589, in Great Burstead, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Francis Chamberlain, was 28 and his mother, Jone Conias, was 30. He married Agnes Haiden in 1619, in England. They were the parents of at least 12 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 1 January 1623, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 34.

Il a épousé Agnes Haiden en 1619, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 12 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 1 janvier 1623, à Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 34 ans.

ko.Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴
ko.lifeSummary: Francis Chamberlain(이)가 1589년 1월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 버스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Francis Chamberlain(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Jone Conias(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1619년 영국에서 Agnes Haiden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1623년 1월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colon: бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Francis Chamberlain родился 1 января 1589 г. в местности грит бурстид, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Francis Chamberlain было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Jone Conias, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Haiden в 1619 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 12 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 1 января 1623 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Francis Chamberlain nació el 1 de enero de 1589, en Great Burstead, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Francis Chamberlain, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Jone Conias, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Agnes Haiden en 1619, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 12 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de enero de 1623, en Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 34 años.
